source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: Asset.lotPrice only uses oracleTimeout to determine if the price is stale.
summary: OracleTimeout is the number of seconds until an oracle value becomes invalid. It is set in the constructor of Asset. And Asset.lotPrice uses OracleTimeout to determine if the saved price is stale. However, OracleTimeout may not be the correct source to determine if the price is stale. Asset.lotPrice may return the incorrect price.
root_cause: Asset.lotPrice relies only on oracleTimeout and misses other staleness/deprecation conditions from Chainlink feeds
broken_invariants: lotPrice must reject prices from deprecated or otherwise invalid oracle feeds, not only old timestamps
trading price validity must include feed health semantics beyond timeout age
oracle adapters should not treat timestamp freshness as sufficient proof of price validity
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: OracleTimeout is the number of seconds until an oracle value becomes invalid. It is set in the constructor of Asset. And Asset.lotPrice uses OracleTimeout to determine if the saved price is stale. However, OracleTimeout may not be the correct source to determine if the price is stale. Asset.lotPrice may return the incorrect price.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; impact, preconditions, or scope are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: Since collaterals have various implementations of price feed. Asset.lotPrice could be modified like: function lotPrice() external view virtual returns (uint192 lotLow, uint192 lotHigh) { … - if (delta <= oracleTimeout) { + if (delta <= actualOracleTimeout()) { lotLow = savedLowPrice; lotHigh = savedHighPrice; … } + function actualOracleTimeout() public view virtual returns (uint192) { + return oracleTimeout; + } Then, collaterals can override actualOracleTimeout to reflect the correct oracle timeout. ### Assessed type Error
